title: First ANEE+ funding granted
content:
First ANEE+ funding granted

tauranne@helsinki.fi Tue, 31/03/2026 - 13:15
The Interdisciplinary Research Network for Ancient Near East (ANEE+) offers funding for events, publications and new project proposals to its members based on applications. Proposals are reviewed monthly by the management team.

title: Irlannin ruokaviennin menestys herättää ihailua
content:
Ireland's food export success inspires admiration

tikklaur@helsinki.fi Mon, 30/03/2026 - 12:33
The team from the Faculty of Agriculture and Forestry at the University of Helsinki explored Ireland's successful food export model in January. The four-day trip to Dublin was an enlightening experience, demonstrating that Finland has much to learn from this green island nation.

title: Ilves kulkee omilla ehdoillaan metsien mosaiikissa
content:
In Finland’s Mosaic of Forests, the Lynx Finds Its Edge

heheiki@helsinki.fi Fri, 27/03/2026 - 13:15
The lynx moves through the mosaic of habitats available in Finland, but still clearly favours forests with deadwood and varying terrain. A new study from the University of Helsinki reveals how skillfully this large predator balances between human-modified areas and natural environments.

Lynx are on the rebound in Finland. Once endangered in

title: Arkadiankadun siltaremontti aiheuttaa muutoksia liikennejärjestelyihin Luonnontieteellisen museon lähistöllä
content:
Renewal of Arkadiankatu Bridge will cause changes to traffic arrangements near the Natural History Museum

johanemi@helsinki.fi Wed, 07/01/2026 - 08:40
The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

The renovation will start with the construction of temporary traffic arrangements from Wednesday 7 January onwards. The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

title: Kotina metsä -näyttely tuo luonnonmetsän keskelle Helsinkiä
content:
Forest Home – Exhibition brings a natural forest to the heart of Helsinki

johanemi@helsinki.fi Fri, 30/05/2025 - 14:09
In Finland, natural forests have become a rarity – and for many, they remain unfamiliar. The Forest Home exhibition invites visitors to discover them through photography, video installations and scientific specimens. Forest Home is on display at the Natural History Museum until 5 April 2026.
